spans.text: ( I'm sure he's right ) There's not a lad in town so bright
to hesitate or falter In primmer , spelling-book or psalter
His genius is too much above it
passion , His parents hold a consultation
From country cares and labour eased
to see with care His church , and farm in good repair
Greek to blunder over a chapter
tells his parents how he learns
much more than he can tell
in little space He'll beat his rivals in the race
in little space He'll beat his rivals in the race
so done , at college now He enters well — no matter how — New scenes awhile his fancy please , But all must yield to love of ease
He enters well — no matter how — New scenes awhile his fancy please
his hours of business double — He can't endure the increasing trouble
the seeds of dire disease : Greek spoils his eyes ( the print's so fine ) Grown dim with study — and with wine
The friend of idleness and ease
The dullness of a lettered brain
The dullness of a lettered brain
Humbly on trust accept the sense
you who boast the name of wise , Wherein substantial learning lies
a charm in sounds of Greek , No language else can learn to speak
No language else can learn to speak
No language else can learn to speak
No language else can learn to speak
language else can learn to speak
, are sense and genius bound To any vehicles of sound
you , who draw the curious line Between the useful and the fine , How little can this noble art It's aid in human things impart , Or give to life a cheerful ray , And force our pains
can this noble art It's aid in human things impart , Or give to life a cheerful ray , And force our pains
can this noble art It's aid in human things impart , Or give to life a cheerful ray , And force our pains
this noble art It's aid in human things impart , Or give to life a cheerful ray
It's aid in human things impart , Or give to life a cheerful ray
whatever was done Above the circle of the sun
Through realms of entity to roam
the secrets dark to scan , Eternal wisdom hid from man
dark to scan , Eternal wisdom hid from man
the sign In din of battle
The glimmering lamp of reason's ray
The glimmering lamp of reason's ray
how proud and vain , While heaven forbids the vaunting strain
For metaphysics rightly shown But teach how little
Though quibbles still maintain their station , Conjecture serves for demonstration , Armies of pens draw forth to fight , And ******* and ****** write
For metaphysics rightly shown But teach how little
For metaphysics rightly shown But teach how little
Armies of pens draw forth to fight , And ******* and ****** write
to see that day , When sense shall point to youths their way
with just discerning Explode the quackeries of learning
their real due , Explain their faults
With taste and fancy well refined , Where moral rapture warms the mind , From schools dismissed , with liberal hand ,
fancy's realms we roam , The main concern
nonsense long , with serious air Has wandered unmolested there ) He passes trial fair , and free , And
commence Without the aid of books or sense : For passing college cures the brain , Like mills to grind men young again
For passing college cures the brain , Like mills to grind men young again
the aid of books or sense
mills to grind men young again
quintessence Of worship , dignity and presence
learning , genius , virtue , worth , Wit , valour , wisdom and so
The cap of folly worn before
with pain His purse as empty as his brain
Where scripture sanctifies his strains , And reverence hides the want of brains
Where dreaded satire may not dare
school appear , Procured for forty pounds a year
throned aloft in elbow-chair , With solemn face and awful air
elbow-chair , With solemn face and awful air
Then throned aloft in elbow-chair , With solemn face and awful air
with ease and unconcern , To teach
with ease and unconcern , To teach
With threats and blows ( incitements pressing ) Drives on his lads to learn each lesson ; Thinks flogging cures all moral ills , And breaks their heads to break their wills
Deride what old divines can say , Point out to heaven a nearer way :
Or on the wings of folly fly Aloft in metaphysic sky ; The system of the world explain , Till night and chaos come again
living works , and dead , Themes , sermons , plans of controversy
What though his learning be so slight
to spell or write ; What though his skull be cudgel-proof
The serious counsels of a friend , Whose utmost wish , in nobler ways , Your sacred dignity to raise
the simple throng around Each kindred blockhead greets the sound
neglecting rule , To aid the triumphs of a fool
it's sacred name , Indulged to man , to point the way
to man , to point the way
Where pure affection warms the mind , While each performs the pious race , Nor dullness ever usurps a place
While human souls are all your care , By warnings
warms the mind , While each performs the pious race , Nor dullness ever usurps a place
the pious race , Nor dullness ever usurps a place
truth , mid rage of dire divisions
The message of the eternal King
Where impudence is yoked to dullness
that spoke at Rome , Or Livy's ox , that to the state Declared the oracles of fate
that spoke at Rome , Or Livy's ox , that to the state Declared the oracles of fate
that to the state Declared the oracles of fate
that spoke at Rome , Or Livy's ox , that to the state Declared the oracles of fate
There deals in preaching and in prayer , And starves on sixty pounds a year , And culls his texts , and tills his farm , And does no good , and little harm
deals in preaching and in prayer , And starves on sixty pounds a year , And culls his texts , and tills his farm , And does no good , and little harm
sunday , in his best array , Deals forth the dullness of the day
while above he spends his breath , The yawning audience
